solution.p: There are a few ways to solve quadratic equations- the easiest way to solve this particular type of problem is to use the square root method.

p.m: \begin{aligned} 43x^2-53 = 0\amp \Rightarrow 43x^2=53 \\ \amp \Rightarrow x^2=\frac{53}{43} \\ \amp \Rightarrow x= -\sqrt{\frac{53}{43}}\text{ or } x=\sqrt{\frac{53}{43}}\\ \amp \Rightarrow x= -\frac{\sqrt{53}}{\sqrt{43}}\cdot\frac{\sqrt{43}}{\sqrt{43}}\text{ or } x=\frac{\sqrt{53}}{\sqrt{43}}\cdot\frac{\sqrt{43}}{\sqrt{43}}\\ \amp \Rightarrow x= -\frac{\sqrt{2279}}{43}\text{ or } x=\frac{\sqrt{2279}}{43}\\ \end{aligned}

solution.p: Note that all quadratic equations have two (possibly repeated) solutions; in this problem both solutions are real. Each solution can be checked by substituting it into the original equation- this is left as an exercise. We rationalized the denominator in each solution by multiplying by a factor of 1 using \frac{\sqrt{43}}{\sqrt{43}}.
